questions.question:
What is the value of F(4) using the following procedure:

function F(K : integer)
integer;

if (k<3) then F:=k else F:=F(k-1)*F(k-2)+F(k-3)
end;

questions.question:
Consider the code fragment written in C below :

void f (int n)
{ 
    if (n <= 1)  {
        printf ("%d", n);
    }
    else {
        f (n/2);
        printf ("%d", n%2);
    }
}
Which of the following implementations will produce the same output for f(173) as the above code?
P1
void f (int n)
{ 
    if (n/2)  {
        f(n/2);
    }
    printf ("%d", n%2);
}

P2
 void f (int n)
{ 
    if (n <=1)  {
        printf ("%d", n);
    }
    else {
        printf ("%d", n%2);
        f (n/2);
    }
}


(GATE IT 2008)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the C program below. What does it print?

 #include < stdio.h >
# define swapl (a, b) tmp = a; a = b; b = tmp
void swap2 ( int a, int b)
{
        int tmp;
        tmp = a; a = b; b = tmp;
 }
void swap3 (int*a, int*b)
{
        int tmp;
        tmp = *a; *a = *b; *b = tmp;
}
int main ()
{
        int num1 = 5, num2 = 4, tmp;
        if (num1 < num2) {swap1 (num1, num2);}
        if (num1 < num2) {swap2 (num1 + 1, num2);}
        if (num1 > = num2) {swap3 (&num1, &num2);}
        printf ("%d, %d", num1, num2);
}


(GATE IT 2008)

questions.question:
Consider the following C functions:

int f1(int n)
{
  if(n == 0 || n == 1)
    return n;
  else
    return (2*f1(n-1) + 3*f1(n-2));
}
 
int f2(int n)
{
  int i;
  int X[N], Y[N], Z[N] ;
  X[0] = Y[0] = Z[0] = 0;
  X[1] = 1; Y[1] = 2; Z[1] = 3;
  for(i = 2; i <= n; i++)
  {
    X[i] = Y[i-1] + Z[i-2];
    Y[i] = 2*X[i];
    Z[i] = 3*X[i];
  }
  return X[n] ;
}

questions.question:
What is printed by the following C program?

 int f(int x, int *py, int **ppz)			
{						                    	
    int y, z;					                		
    **ppz += 1; z = **ppz;		                
    *py += 2; y = *py;					                	
    x += 3;					                    	
    return x+y+z;
}

void main()
{
    int c, *b, **a;
    c = 4; b = &c; a = &b;
    printf("%d", f(c, b, a));
    	
}

questions.question:
Consider the program below in a hypothetical language which allows global variable and a choice of call by reference or call by value methods of parameter passing. 

int i ;
program main ()
{
    int j = 60;
    i = 50;
    call f (i, j);
    print i, j;
}
procedure f (x, y)
{           
    i = 100;
    x = 10;
    y = y + i ;
}

Which one of the following options represents the correct output of the program for the two parameter passing mechanisms?

(GATE IT 2007)
options:
A.

Call by value : i = 70, j = 10; Call by reference :i = 60, j = 70

B.

Call by value : i = 50, j = 60; Call by reference :i = 50, j = 70

C.

Call by value : i = 10, j = 70; Call by reference :i = 100, j = 60

D.

Call by value : i = 100, j = 60; Call by reference :i = 10, j = 70
questions.correct_answer:
D.

Call by value : i = 100, j = 60; Call by reference :i = 10, j = 70
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
The function f is defined as follows:

 int f (int n) {
    if (n <= 1) return 1;
    else if (n % 2  ==  0) return f(n/2);
    else return f(3n - 1);
}

Assuming that arbitrarily large integers can be passed as a parameter to the function, consider the following statements.

i. The function f terminates for finitely many different values of \(n \geq 1\).
ii. The function f terminates for infinitely many different values of \(n \geq 1\).
iii. The function f does not terminate for finitely many different values of \(n \geq 1\).
iv. The function f does not terminate for infinitely many different values of \(n \geq 1\).

Which one of the following options is true of the above?

questions.question:
Consider the following C function:

 #include < stdio.h >
int f(int n)
{
    static int r = 0;
    if (n <= 0) return 1;
    if (n > 3)
    {
        r = n;
        return f(n-2)+2;
    }
    return f(n-1)+r;
}
 
int main()
{
    printf("%d", f(5));
}
What is the value of f(5)?

 int func(int m, int n)

questions.question:
Which one of the choices given below would be printed when the following program is executed?

#include < stdio.h >
void swap (int *x, int *y)
{
    static int *temp;
    temp = x;
    x = y;
    y = temp;
}
void printab ()
{
    static int i, a = -3, b = -6;
    i = 0;
    while (i <= 4)
    {
        if ((i++)%2 == 1) continue;
        a = a + i;
        b = b + i;
    }
    swap (&a, &b);
    printf("a =  %d, b = %d\n", a, b);
}
main()
{
    printab();
    printab();
}


(GATE IT 2006)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow
